meaning: n.短棒,护身用手杖v.以短棒攻击
examples: Our side was under the cosh for most of the second half.
The home team was soon under the cosh.
And it's not just the chemicals in the brain that cosh your resident Romeo.
Under the cosh, Munster rallied superbly and hit back with the last move of the half.
MPs are under his cosh and, after a stunning election victory, most party members are behind him.
